channel.title: RSS Aggregator Feed
channel.description: An aggregated feed of Technology-related news

item.title: Hackers are abusing unpatched Windows security flaws to hack into organizations
item.description: A security researcher published details of three security vulnerabilities in Windows Defender, and the code used to exploit them. Now, hackers are taking advantage of the vulnerabilities in real life attacks, according to a cybersecurity firm.

item.title: Train-to-Test scaling explained: How to optimize your end-to-end AI compute budget for inference
item.description: The standard guidelines for building large language models (LLMs) optimize only for training costs and ignore inference costs. This poses a challenge for real-world applications that use inference-time scaling techniques to increase the accuracy of model responses, such as drawing multiple reasoning samples from a model at deployment.To bridge this gap, researchers at University of Wisconsin-Madison and Stanford University have introduced Train-to-Test (T2) scaling laws, a framework that jointly optimizes a model’s parameter size, its training data volume, and the number of test-time inference...

item.title: This charming gadget writes bad AI poetry
item.description: I've never been as charmed and frustrated by one gadget as I have with the Poetry Camera. It's a delightful object. White and cherry red with a color-matched woven strap, it looks playful and adorably lo-fi. If I saw it on a store shelf, I'd absolutely pick it up. But aside from obviously appealing, I'm […]

item.title: Zoom teams up with World to verify humans in meetings
item.description: Zoom will show a badge on verified participants' tile.

item.title: Sam Altman's 'human verification' company thinks its eye-scanning orbs could solve ticket scalping
item.description: ,Among them, is a new tool called Concert Kit that could help bands and artists fight back against ticket scalping bots. The new feature relies on the revamped World ID, the orb-based verification system that scans users eyeballs and faces to create a "proof of human" signature that lives on users' mobile devices. "It's basically like a little human passport for the internet that lets you prove on apps and websites that you are a real and unique human without revealing anything about yourself," Tools for Humanity Chief Product Officer Tiago Sada tells Engadget. Now, as more apps and services a...

item.title: Gazing Into Sam Altman’s Orb Now Proves You’re Human on Tinder
item.description: Honestly, what’s hotter than a real person?

item.title: MG4 à batterie semi-solide : avancée majeure ou mirage marketing ? Les ingénieurs s’expliquent
item.description: Les batteries solides sont attendues comme le graal des batteries pour voitures électriques. Cependant, leur mise en production demande beaucoup de temps avant d’aboutir à un produit fiable et à un prix raisonnable. MG a alors trouvé un compromis : la batterie semi-solide, qui arrive dès cette année en Europe sur la MG4. On a pu discuter avec des ingénieurs chinois responsables du programme. ...

item.title: Most enterprises can't stop stage-three AI agent threats, VentureBeat survey finds
item.description: A rogue AI agent at Meta passed every identity check and still exposed sensitive data to unauthorized employees in March. Two weeks later, Mercor, a $10 billion AI startup, confirmed a supply-chain breach through LiteLLM. Both are traced to the same structural gap. Monitoring without enforcement, enforcement without isolation. A VentureBeat three-wave survey of 108 qualified enterprises found that the gap is not an edge case. It is the most common security architecture in production today.Gravitee’s State of AI Agent Security 2026 survey of 919 executives and practitioners quantifies the disco...

item.title: 'They mopped the floor with me and pulled every childish game they could': Disgruntled researcher releases second major Windows zero-day — claims Microsoft 'would ruin my life, and they did'
item.description: Chaotic Eclipse is at it again, this time releasing a zero-day for Microsoft Defender.

item.title: Will there be a Something Very Bad Is Going to Happen Season 2?
item.description: Can "Something Very Bad Is Going to Happen" viewers expect more Rachel Harkin (Camila Morrone) in Season 2?

item.title: Gigs turns your concert history into a personal live music archive
item.description: New iPhone app Gigs uses AI to turn old tickets, screenshots, and emails into a personal concert archive with stats, memories, and more.

item.title: Bluesky blames DDoS attack for server outages
item.description: Bluesky is once again having a wobble. The platform said some of its systems are down and that it’s “investigating an incident with service in one of our reginos” (that’s Bluesky’s typo, not mine). The issue appears to have started at 1:42AM ET and was still persisting as of 11AM when this story was originally published. Since then, the site has been experiencing intermitent interuptions, including at times to its status page where users should be able to monitor outages. At 7:47PM ET, the platform explained that it’s been attempting to mitigate “a sophisticated Distributed Denial-of-Service (...

item.title: Where can you stream Something Very Bad Is Going to Happen?
item.description: Here's where you can catch the spooky miniseries starring Camila Morrone and Adam DiMarco, "Something Very Bad Is Going to Happen."

item.title: Xiaomi Poco M7, trottinette Xiaomi Electric Scooter 5 Pro et Huawei Watch GT Runner 2 : les meilleurs bons plans du jour (17 avril 2026)
item.description: Ce vendredi 17 avril 2026, les remises s’enchaînent chez les principaux marchands comme Amazon, la Fnac et Boulanger.

item.title: Hbada X7 Smart review: Excuse me, but since when do office chairs heat, massage, and cool you at the touch of a button?
item.description: This office chair just might have the most features of any I've ever tested.

item.title: Transformer nos plastiques en hydrogène vert : l’incroyable découverte de chercheurs de Cambridge
item.description: Et si les déchets plastiques devenaient une ressource énergétique ? Des chercheurs britanniques ont mis au point un système capable de produire de l’hydrogène à partir de plastiques usagés et d’acide recyclé.

item.title: Bluesky breaks silence on outage and reveals cause
item.description: Bluesky, the anti-X social network, suffered an outage on April 16. Now, the cause has been revealed.

item.title: Chef Robotics escaped the robot cooking graveyard and says it’s thriving — here’s why
item.description: The company, which deploys AI-guided robot arms for food production, says it is looking to expand its services to provide for a broader array of customers.

item.title: Marvel Rivals now finally lets us play as Black Cat, but her awful posture makes me worry for her back — 'I just know her spine is killing her!'
item.description: The latest Marvel Rivals season update has finally arrived alongside the addition of Black Cat, but it's her character selection pose that has players talking.

item.title: NASA restarts work to support Europe's uncrewed trip to Mars after years of setbacks
item.description: NASA has confirmed the pending launch of the European Space Agency's (ESA) Rosalind Franklin rover, which is being sent to Mars. The current plan is to launch via a SpaceX Falcon Heavy rocket from Kennedy Space Center. The timing is still being worked out, but the space agency says this won't happen until at least 2028. This is a partnership between NASA and the ESA, with the European agency providing the rover, the spacecraft and the lander. The US will provide braking engines for the lander, heater units for the rover's internal systems and, of course, assistance with the actual launch. The ...

item.title: Il vole 10 000 dollars à MKBHD grâce à Apple Pay sans même déverrouiller son iPhone
item.description: Une faille d'Apple Pay et du réseau Visa ont permis de voler avec une facilité déconcertante 10 000 dollars à MKBHD dans le cadre d'une vidéo de démo. Voici comment fonctionne l'arnaque.

item.title: Rachat à 1,1 centime le kWh : l’État signe-t-il la fin des panneaux solaires pour les particuliers ?
item.description: L'État semble vouloir définitivement tourner la page de la revente de surplus d'électricité solaire. Les soutiens aux petites installations pourraient connaître une nouvelle fois une modification radicale. Ceux qui envisagent d'installer des panneaux photovoltaïques devront désormais prévoir une batterie de stockage.

item.title: YouTube watchers praise ‘awesome’ new option to disable annoying Shorts — here’s how to do it
item.description: YouTube finally has an option for users that will allow them to effectively remove Shorts via a zero-minute timer.

item.title: Panic says the Playdate Catalog won't accept games made with generative AI
item.description: Panic, the company behind the tiny and excellent Playdate console, is taking a stand on generative AI. The company has published an AI disclosure that says as of this month, the Playdate Catalog “will no longer accept titles that use ‘Generative AI’ for art, audio, music, text, or dialog.” Panic does allow for developers to use AI assistance for coding, but also says that “we will flag any title as such and specify the extent that it was used (for example, “Lua debugging”) so the customer can decide whether to support it or not.”This comes a day after Panic announced that Playdate season three...

item.title: ‘Go to hell’: Bernie Sanders unites labor leaders in huge push for AI protections and a halt to data center construction – growing national anti-data center sentiment results in protests, bans, and project cancellations
item.description: Bernie Sanders joined labor leaders to warn of the threat the US workforce is facing from AI.

item.title: Comment brancher sa guitare, son clavier électronique et son micro sur ordinateur ?
item.description: Vous voulez connecter votre guitare, votre micro ou tout autre instrument à votre ordinateur ? Découvrez les étapes pour brancher votre instrument de musique sur PC ou Mac, les astuces et les produits indispensables pour une expérience sonore optimale.

item.title: Cette météorite dormait dans la terre australienne depuis moins de 1 000 ans et personne ne l'avait remarquée
item.description: Une roche trouvée par hasard en Australie s'est révélée être un fragment vieux de 4,6 milliards d'années. Un chasseur de pépites pensait tenir de l'or, il tenait en réalité un morceau du Système solaire primitif. Comment une météorite aussi ancienne a-t-elle pu rester enfouie dans une forêt...

item.title: Our new favorite budget phones
item.description: Over the last few months, every phone maker under the sun has refreshed its lineups, especially at the affordable end, with no major budget phone launches now expected until next year. That made it the perfect time to go back and reassess The Verge's recommendations for the best budget phones you can buy right now. […]

item.title: The LG C5 is our top-rated TV and Amazon's best-selling OLED display — get the 42-inch model for only $999
item.description: The LG C5 is our best-rated TV and Amazon's number 1 best-seller in OLED displays — you can now get the 42-inch model on sale for only $999.

item.title: TCL is adding three more new mini-LED and QLED models to its 2026 TV lineup, and one of my favourite budget models I tested last year is continuing
item.description: TCL adds more TVs to its 2026 lineup, including another mini-LED and cheaper QLED models.

item.title: The best budget smartphones you can buy
item.description: Some of us take a kind of “eat to live” rather than a “live to eat” approach to gadgets. They’re tools that help you get things done, not something you want to invest a lot of time or money in. If that’s you — and there’s no judgment here from a certifiable gadget nerd — […]

item.title: 'This is not fun and games…’: OpenAI exec says AI 'doomers' are holding back the ‘incredible economic opportunities’ the tech can provide for ‘families and for society writ large’
item.description: OpenAI's policy exec really wants to convince people that the benefits of AI are real and tangible
